title: Feat/add force capability on export
body:
## 📝 Summary

This pull request introduces a --force / -f flag to the marimo export subcommands. This allows users to overwrite existing output files, which is essential for automation and scripting use cases.

Fixes #5523

## 🔍 Description of Changes

Currently, when marimo export is used with an --output argument that points to an existing file, it interactively prompts the user for confirmation before overwriting. While this is a safe default for manual use, it blocks non-interactive workflows, such as in CI/CD pipelines or automated scripts.

This PR resolves the issue by:

Adding a --force and -f option to all relevant export commands (html, script, md, ipynb, and html_wasm).

Updating the watch_and_export function to check for this flag. If force is True, the prompt_to_overwrite confirmation step is skipped, and the file is overwritten directly.

Adding a new pytest test case (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) to ensure the new functionality works as expected. The test verifies that:

Attempting to export to an existing file without the flag does not overwrite it.

Using --force successfully overwrites the existing file.

Using the short alias -f also works correctly.

These changes make the export feature more versatile and friendly for automated environments without altering the safe default behavior for interactive users.

step_plan.summary: Add a `--force/-f` flag to all `marimo export` subcommands and plumb it through the shared `watch_and_export` helper so existing output files can be overwritten without interactive prompts. This preserves the current safe default while enabling non-interactive automation workflows.

steps.operation: Add a `force: bool = False` parameter to the shared export helper and bypass overwrite confirmation when enabled
steps.reason: `watch_and_export` centralizes writing to `--output` and currently gates overwriting behind `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)`. Adding a `force` switch here ensures all export subcommands get consistent overwrite behavior and that `--watch` mode also skips the one-time prompt when `--force` is set.
steps.side_effects: Changes the helper’s call signature; all callers must be updated to pass `force` (or rely on the default). Behavior change only when `force=True`; otherwise prompts/guards remain. Any tests that mock or assert overwrite prompting may need updates.

steps.operation: Add `--force/-f` Click option to HTML export and thread it into the helper call
steps.reason: Expose the new overwrite control to users for `marimo export html` and ensure it reaches `watch_and_export` so the prompt is skipped and the file is overwritten when requested.
steps.side_effects: CLI surface change (new flag). Help text/option parsing changes; downstream automation can now use `--force/-f`. Must ensure option ordering/Click decorators remain consistent with existing options.

steps.operation: Add `--force/-f` Click option to script export and pass through to `watch_and_export`
steps.reason: Keep feature parity across export formats; `marimo export script` must support non-interactive overwrites the same way as HTML.
steps.side_effects: CLI surface change for this subcommand; requires updating the call to `watch_and_export` to include `force=force` (or equivalent), otherwise the option would be ignored.

steps.operation: Add `--force/-f` Click option to markdown export and pass through to `watch_and_export`
steps.reason: Enable overwrite-without-prompt for `marimo export md`, consistent with other exporters and necessary for scripted docs generation.
steps.side_effects: CLI option addition; ensure `--output` + `--force` works both for stdout vs file outputs and does not alter default prompting when `--force` is absent.

steps.operation: Add `--force/-f` Click option to ipynb export and pass through to `watch_and_export`
steps.reason: Allow automation that regenerates notebooks to overwrite existing `.ipynb` outputs without interactive confirmation.
steps.side_effects: CLI surface change; update helper invocation accordingly. If there are any ipynb-specific validations, ensure they still run before overwriting occurs.

steps.operation: Add `--force/-f` Click option to html-wasm export and ensure the computed output HTML path uses the `force` behavior
steps.reason: `html_wasm` may derive the final HTML file path from an output directory and also writes additional assets. The force flag must be applied to the final HTML write (via `watch_and_export`) so users can overwrite existing wasm HTML outputs without prompts.
steps.side_effects: May change behavior when output HTML already exists: with `--force` it will overwrite without confirmation. Ensure any pre-write asset generation doesn’t unintentionally clobber directories/files unexpectedly; if assets are written before the final HTML overwrite check, consider whether they should also respect force semantics.
